summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Graphs of Trigonometric Functions
summary_sections.content_html:
Sine Function: y = sin(x) has amplitude 1, period 2ĂŹâ‚¬. Oscillates between -1 and 1. Passes through origin (0, 0). Key points: (ĂŹâ‚¬/2, 1), (ĂŹâ‚¬, 0), (3ĂŹâ‚¬/2, -1), (2ĂŹâ‚¬, 0).

Cosine Function: y = cos(x) has amplitude 1, period 2ĂŹâ‚¬. Key points: (0, 1), (ĂŹâ‚¬/2, 0), (ĂŹâ‚¬, -1), (3ĂŹâ‚¬/2, 0), (2ĂŹâ‚¬, 1). Shifts sine left by ĂŹâ‚¬/2: cos(x) = sin(x + ĂŹâ‚¬/2).

Transformations: y = A sin(B(x - C)) + D. A is amplitude (vertical stretch). B affects period (new period = 2ĂŹâ‚¬/B). C is horizontal shift. D is vertical shift. Tangent has period ĂŹâ‚¬, not 2ĂŹâ‚¬.

Tangent Function: y = tan(x) = sin(x)/cos(x). Undefined at x = ĂŹâ‚¬/2, 3ĂŹâ‚¬/2, ... (where cos = 0). Vertical asymptotes at these points. Period = ĂŹâ‚¬.
